method_card.parent_question: How can chunked/source-span LLM methods preserve source grounding while still supporting useful abstraction?
intended_inputs: operator-authorized third source excerpt: a 950-word volatility-regime and tool-selection teaching segment from a different transcript than sources one and two
metadata-safe source hash and source ref for the selected excerpt, with locally wrapped 50-character line structure recorded as part of selection
the pilot 006 line-range-first locator contract unchanged
DeepSeek V4 Pro with thinking enabled and a reasoning-aware 12000-token cap
pre-recorded prompt and model configuration metadata
intended_outputs: locally computed quote hashes from validated spans
known_risks: This is a proposed live LLM pilot planning record, not a completed run.
ASR degradation in the third source (missing punctuation, garbled words) may reduce claim faithfulness or make locator review harder.
Thinking mode ignored entry caps in long-context pilot 004; local validation must enforce caps.
The model may emit line ranges that are broad, nearby, off-by-one, or confidence-overstated rather than support-valid.
The model must not emit character offsets or quote hashes; local review computes them only after line-range validation.
Prompt or trace handling may accidentally include raw source material if the privacy boundary is not followed.
